previous_attempts.reason: set -u + unset LD_LIBRARY_PATH in conda activate hook
torch 1.13 doesn't support sm_90 (H100); upgraded to torch 2.1+cu121
pooled_stats lambda for p95 didn't accept axis= kwarg; fixed all 4 stat fns to take a single arg
I/O bottleneck: cap.set(POS_FRAMES) per video frame over NFS = ~100 s/clip; rewrote to sequential read (single forward pass through video, ~5-10 s/clip)

stats.fail_reason: no face_track_cache or no usable track

jobs.name: avhubert_real_late_fusion_lr

audio_only.note: identical to spec-015 substitute baseline (audio path unchanged)

jobs.verdict: NEGATIVE-DEEPENED. Acceptance criterion (always_fuse OR gated_av AUROC >= audio_only 0.831) FAILED — best fusion AUROC is 0.708, ~0.12 below audio_only. Real AV-HuBERT-Large embeddings perform WORSE than the spec-015 hand-engineered substitute (-0.047 AUROC on always_fuse, -0.025 on gated_av). Diagnosis: visual-only LR shows extreme val/test gap (val AUROC 0.981 → test 0.667), classic overfit on 4096+9 features against 431 val clips. The richer feature space does not generalize across the seen-child split. Implication: the spec-015 finding (`visual fusion hurts the audio-only baseline on this task`) is NOT a substitute-feature artifact — it persists with proper visual-speech embeddings. Visual modality is not the missing ingredient here; the audio pseudo-frame baseline is already capturing whatever visual information would help.

previous_attempts.reason: set -u + unset LD_LIBRARY_PATH in conda activate hook

jobs.name: build_split_and_train_wavlm_mil_knnvc
jobs.verdict: STRONG NEGATIVE. Model collapsed to predict-all-positive (F1=trivial-positive floor of 0.86; AUROC near-random 0.54). Diagnosis: HiFiGAN vocoder signature overfit — the converted audio carries an artifact pattern that the WavLM-MIL backbone latches on as a positive marker, but val/test are real audio so the artifact is absent and the model has no actual class signal. Confirms the plan's risk-register concern (likelihood medium-high). Echoes the TinyVox padding-shortcut precedent: synth signal that's separable from real on a non-task-relevant axis poisons clip-level training. Implication: frame-level voice transfer alone does NOT close the spec-016 follow-up #1 NEUTRAL — the binding constraint is artifact-realism, not voice-identity-fidelity. T160 (cross-child variant) is SKIPPED per the conditional in T160.

summary.us1_verdict: NEGATIVE-DEEPENED. Real AV-HuBERT-Large LR fusion is worse than spec-015 hand-engineered substitute (best fusion test AUROC 0.708 vs substitute always_fuse 0.755 / gated_av 0.733; both ~-0.12 below audio_only 0.831). Visual modality fails harder with richer features due to LR overfit (val AUROC 0.981 → test 0.667 with 4105 features × 431 val clips).
summary.us2_verdict: STRONG NEGATIVE. WavLM-MIL on KNN-VC-converted positives collapsed to predict-all-positive (test AUROC 0.536 vs WavLM-MIL real baseline 0.771; ΔAUROC -0.235). HiFiGAN vocoder signature overfit confirmed.
summary.joint_verdict: Both spec-017 attack vectors (richer visual features, frame-level voice transfer) bombed. The spec-015 substitute and spec-016 follow-up #1 NEUTRAL results were NOT artifacts of impoverished implementations — they reflect real saturation. (a) Visual modality is not missing signal that proper AV-HuBERT can supply; the audio pseudo-frame baseline already captures what visual would. (b) Voice-transfer-as-augmentation fails on a different axis than mean-pooling: KNN-VC's HiFiGAN signature is too distinct from real audio. Both attack vectors in §18.7.4 (i) and (ii) are now closed; remaining viable direction is (iii) localization on the synth holdout.
tasks_skipped: T160 (cross-child US2; conditional on T152 POSITIVE — T152 was strong NEGATIVE)
